title: Verb Conjugation Patterns
description: Learn how to form Spanish verbs in the 'to' form and the 'I' form, focusing on regular and some irregular verbs.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say 'to ventilate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Ventilar. Good. How would you say 'to separate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Separar. Good. Remember, the 'a' is pronounced as in 'apple'. How would you say 'to know'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Saber. Good. And 'to come'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Venir. Good. Spanish verbs in the 'to' form end in -ar, -er, or -ir. For example, 'to want' is querer. How do you say 'I want'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero. Good. How about 'I owe' or 'I must'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Debo. Good. And 'I try'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Intento. Good. Remember, regular verbs change from -ar, -er, or -ir to -o in the 'I' form. How would you say 'to segregate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Segregar. Good. Now, how do you say 'to breathe'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Respirar. Good. So, 'to know' is saber. How do you say 'I know'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Sé. Good. 'To come' is venir. How do you say 'I come'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Vengo. Good. Remember, irregular verbs like tener and venir have unique forms. How would you say 'to donate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Donar. And 'I donate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Dono. Good. How about 'to create'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Crear. And 'I create'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Creo. Good. 'To eat' is comer. How do you say 'I eat'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Como. Good. How would you say 'I'm going to eat late'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Voy a comer tarde. Good. And 'I'm going to eat tomorrow'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Voy a comer mañana. Remember, you can use the present tense with a future context. How would you say 'I eat tomorrow'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Como mañana. Good. How do you say 'I eat with Pablo tomorrow'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Como con Pablo mañana. Good. How would you say 'I want to have it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero tenerlo. Good. And 'I'm going to have it later'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Voy a tenerlo más tarde. Good. Remember, 'más' means 'more', so 'más tarde' is 'later'. How would you say 'to obtain'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Obtener. Good. And 'to sustain'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Sostener. Good. How about 'to maintain'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Mantener. Good. And 'to entertain'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Entretener. Good. 'I have' is tengo. How would you say 'I come tomorrow'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Vengo mañana. Very good.

outro.text: You've learned how to form Spanish verbs in the 'to' form and the 'I' form, focusing on regular and some irregular verbs.
Try forming 'I' forms from other verbs you know, like 'to study' or 'to write'.
Remember, regular verbs change from -ar, -er, or -ir to -o in the 'I' form, and some irregular verbs have unique forms.
